attributes.markupText:
Private Rented Sector Exemptions Privacy Notice

This notice sets out how we will process your personal data, and your information rights. It is made under Articles 13 and/or 14 of the UK General Data Protection Regulation (UK GDPR).

The Private Rented Sector (PRS) Exemptions Register service manages the exemption process for landlords who demonstrate a just reason not to achieve the minimum 'E' Energy Performance Certificate (EPC) rating for renting domestic and non-domestic properties.

1. Your data

We will process the following personal data:

  • the name, address, email address and telephone number of the landlord (individual or organisation)
  • the name, business address, email address and telephone number of the landlord agent, when a landlord agent has registered an exemption on the landlord's behalf
  • the address of the property to be exempt
  • other information relevant to the exemption (according to the minimum standard regulations)

If you use our Assisted Digital (AD) telephone support service, we will also process:

  • call audio recordings
  • call handler notes
  • information you provide during the call to help you complete your exemption
  • any identity verification details you provide verbally
  • The AD service is delivered on behalf of DESNZ by HGS UK, who acts as a data processor.

2. Purpose

The purpose(s) for which we are processing your personal data are:

    • The Department for Energy Security and Net Zero (DESNZ) processes your personal data under the Energy Efficiency (Private Rented Property) (England and Wales) Regulations 2015 in order to administer and enforce the Minimum Energy Efficiency Standards (MEES).

For users of the Assisted Digital service,

we process your personal data to:

  • provide support to complete exemption applications on your behalf
  • ensure accuracy of information entered into the PRS Exemptions Register
  • maintain call recordings for quality assurance, training, audit, and fraud prevention
  • provide a record of advice provided during the call

Main purposes of processing

The main purposes of processing are to:

  • Enable landlords of domestic and non-domestic properties to comply with the minimum energy efficiency standards by registering valid exemptions.
  • Allow local authorities to review exemptions, contact landlords or agents, and undertake compliance and enforcement activity.
  • Enable the government and local authorities to monitor performance and enforce the regulations, in order to improve the overall energy efficiency and quality of the private rented sector.
  • Support research, evaluation, and statistical analysis relating to the operation of the MEES regulations, housing quality, and energy efficiency policies.
  • We may also use your information for research and evaluation purposes relating to the minimum standards regulations, the operation of the register, and other DESNZ energy efficiency programmes. Data used for these purposes will be anonymised wherever possible.
  • We may use your data for compiling statistical reports relating to the minimum standards regulations. This may include linking the data you provide to Energy Company Obligation (ECO) delivery data or other government held datasets. Information used for these purposes will be anonymised.
  • We may use your email address to send you service-related notices (including any notices required by law, in lieu of communication by postal mail).

3. Legal basis of processing

The legal basis for processing your personal data under Article 6 of the UK GDPR is:

Legal obligation Article 6(1)(c) – Processing is necessary for compliance with a legal obligation to which DESNZ is subject under The Energy Efficiency (Private Rented Property) (England and Wales) Regulations 2015, made under the Energy Act 2011.

These regulations require DESNZ to maintain and manage the PRS Exemptions Register and share information with enforcement authorities in order to administer and enforce compliance with the minimum energy efficiency standards.

This includes enabling landlords renting domestic or non-domestic properties under the relevant tenancy types with an Energy Performance Certificate (EPC) rating below 'E' to register exemptions and to provide the information to the relevant enforcement bodies, local authorities or trading standards.

Public task Article 6(1)(e): Processing is necessary for the performance of a task carried out in the public interest or in the exercise of official authority vested in the data controller, to exercise its functions as a government department.

The specific public task is to allow landlords to submit an exemption from the Energy Efficiency (Private Rented Property) (England and Wales) Regulations 2015 and allow Local Authorities and Local Weights and Measures to review the information submitted for compliance and enforcement purposes.

4. Recipients

Your personal data will be shared by us with the relevant enforcement authority to enable them to enforce compliance with the minimum standard regulations.

This may include sharing your personal data with:

  • for non-domestic rental properties — the Local Weights and Measures Authority within whose boundaries the rental property sits
  • for domestic rental properties — the Local Authority within whose boundaries the rental property sits

Information submitted to the register may be shared with the DESNZ Statistical Unit for the purposes of compiling statistical reports relating to the minimum standards regulations. This may include linking the data you provide to Energy Company Obligation (ECO) delivery data or other government held datasets. Information used for these purposes will be anonymised.

Personal data may be shared with third-party organisations commissioned by the Department for Energy Security and Net Zero (DESNZ) to carry out research, evaluation, or statistical analysis relating to the minimum energy efficiency standards or the operation of the PRS Exemptions Register.

These organisations may include other government departments such as the Ministry of Housing, Communities & Local Government (MHCLG) where projects relate to housing or energy policy development. [landlord database]

Any data shared for research or evaluation will be minimised and anonymised wherever possible. Your identifiable contact details will only be shared if you have not objected to being contacted for research purposes.

Additional sharing in Wales

For rental properties located in Wales, personal data may also be shared with Rent Smart Wales.

Rent Smart Wales is the licensing and enforcement authority for landlords and agents in Wales. Personal data may be shared where necessary to support the administration, monitoring, and enforcement of the minimum energy efficiency standards and related housing regulations.

Data shared with Rent Smart Wales will be limited to what is necessary for these purposes and will be handled in accordance with UK data protection legislation.

If you do not want your personal information to be used in research work undertaken on behalf of DESNZ by a third party, please email us at prsregistersupport@energysecurity.gov.uk.

If you use the Assisted Digital service, your personal data will also be shared with our contracted AD provider, Hinduja Global Solutions UK Limited (HGS), who delivers the telephone support service on behalf of DESNZ. This includes access to call recordings and call notes.

The AD provider acts as a data processor under contract to DESNZ.

The public register

The minimum standard regulations require the following information to be published on the public page of the register:

  • the address of the exempt property
  • the name of the landlord (where the landlord is not an individual)
  • the exemption category
  • the energy performance certificate for the property
  • the date on which the exemption was registered

Publication of penalties

Where a landlord is in breach of the minimum standard regulations and a penalty notice has been issued, the minimum standards regulations give powers to enforcement authorities to publish the following information on the register:

  • the landlord's name (where the landlord is not an individual)
  • details of the breach of the regulations
  • the address of the property
  • the amount of any financial penalty imposed

The minimum standard regulations require that this information be published on the penalties page for a minimum period of 12 months.

As part of our IT infrastructure, your personal data will be stored in the UK on systems provided by our data processors – Microsoft, Amazon Web Services and Salesforce. This does not mean we actively share your personal data with these entities; rather, they are technical service providers who host infrastructure supporting our IT systems

5. Retention

DESNZ will store your personal data for 7 years after an exemption expires. DESNZ may store the personal data beyond that date for statistical purposes.

Call recordings and call notes for the Assisted Digital Service are kept for 2 years for training, audit, and fraud prevention.

6. Automated decisions making

Your personal data will not be subject to automated decision making.

7. International transfers

Your personal data will be processed and stored in the United Kingdom and the EU.

Your data is held on secure systems provided by our data processors — Microsoft, Amazon Web Services (AWS), and Salesforce. These services host data for DESNZ within the United Kingdom, including Microsoft Exchange (email), Teams, SharePoint, OneDrive, and Salesforce for customer relationship and exemption register management.

Salesforce's backup and recovery services are provided by Own Backup, whose data centres are located in the European Union (EU). Transfers of data to these centres are protected by the UK–EU adequacy decision, which recognises EU data protection law as providing an equivalent level of protection to that in the UK.

8. Your rights

You have the right to request information about how your personal data are processed, and to request a copy of that personal data.

You have the right to request that any inaccuracies in your personal data are rectified without delay.

You have the right to request that any incomplete personal data are completed, including by means of a supplementary statement.

You have the right to request that your personal data are erased if there is no longer a justification for them to be processed.

You have the right in certain circumstances (for example, where accuracy is contested) to request that the processing of your personal data is restricted.

You have the right to object to the processing of your personal data where it is processed for direct marketing purposes.

You have the right to object to the processing of your personal data.

To exercise your rights please contact the Data Protection Officer using the contact details below.

9. Contact details

The data controller for your personal data is the Department for Energy Security and Net Zero (DESNZ). You can contact the DESNZ Data Protection Officer at:

DESNZ Data Protection Officer
Department for Energy Security and Net Zero
3-8 Whitehall Place
London
SW1A 2EG

Email: dataprotection@energysecurity.gov.uk

If you are unhappy with the way we have handled your personal data, please write to the department's Data Protection Officer in the first instance using the contact details above.

10. Complaints

If you consider that your personal data has been misused or mishandled, you may make a complaint to the Information Commissioner, who is an independent regulator. The Information Commissioner can be contacted at:

Information Commissioner's Office
Wycliffe House
Water Lane
Wilmslow
Cheshire
SK9 5AF

Telephone: 0303 123 1113

https://ico.org.uk/make-a-complaint/

Any complaint to the Information Commissioner is without prejudice to your right to seek redress through the courts.

11. Updates to this notice

If this privacy notice changes in any way, we will place an updated version on this page. Regularly reviewing this page ensures you are always aware of what information we collect, how we use it, and under what circumstances we will share it with other parties. The 'last updated' date at the bottom of this page will also change.

If these changes affect how your personal data is processed, we will take reasonable steps to let you know.

Published: 23 March 2026

Last updated: 23 March 2026
